elements.sent-2411-001: Let $\alpha(G)$ denote the cardinality of a maximum independent set and $\mu(G)$ be the size of a maximum matching of a graph $G=\left( V,E\right) $.
elements.sent-2411-002: If $\alpha(G)+\mu(G)=\left\vert V\right\vert $, then $G$ is a K\"{onig-Egerv\'{a ry graph , and $G$ is a $1$ -K\"{onig-Egerv\'{a ry graph whenever $\alpha(G)+\mu(G)=\left\vert V\right\vert -1$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-003: The corona $H\circ\mathcal{X}$ of a graph $H$ and a family of graphs $\mathcal{X =\left\{ X_{i :1\leq i\leq\left\vert V(H)\right\vert \right\} $ is obtained by joining each vertex $v_{i}$ of $H$ to all the vertices of the corresponding graph $X_{i ,i=1,2,...,\left\vert V(H)\right\vert $.}}}
elements.sent-2411-004: In this paper we completely characterize graphs whose coronas are $k$-K\"{o nig-Egerv\'{a ry graphs, where $k\in\left\{ 0,1\right\} $.}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Keywords: maximum independent set, maximum matching, K\"{o nig-Egerv\'{a ry graph, $1$-K\"{o nig-Egerv\'{a ry graph, corona of graphs.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: Throughout this paper $G=(V,E)$ is a finite, undirected, loopless graph without multiple edges, with vertex set $V=V(G)$ of cardinality $\left\vert V\left( G\right) \right\vert =n\left( G\right) $, and edge set $E=E(G)$ of size $\left\vert E\left( G\right) \right\vert =m\left( G\right) $.
elements.sent-2411-007: If $X\subset V$, then $G[X]$ is the subgraph of $G$ induced by $X$.
elements.sent-2411-008: By $G-v$ we mean the subgraph $G[V-\left\{ v\right\} ]$, for $v\in V$.
elements.sent-2411-009: The neighborhood of a vertex $v\in V$ is the set $N(v)=\{w:w\in V$ and $vw\in E\}$.
elements.sent-2411-010: The neighborhood of $A\subseteq V$ is $N(A)=\{v\in V:N(v)\cap A\neq\emptyset\}$.
elements.sent-2411-011: A set $S\subseteq V(G)$ is independent if no two vertices belonging to $S$ are adjacent.
elements.sent-2411-012: The independence number $\alpha(G)$ is the size of a largest independent set (i.e., of a maximum independent set) of $G$.
elements.sent-2411-013: Let $\mathrm{core (G)$ be the intersection of all maximum independent sets of $G$.}
elements.sent-2411-014: A matching in a graph $G$ is a set of edges $M\subseteq E\left( G\right) $ such that no two edges of $M$ share a common vertex.
elements.sent-2411-015: A matching of maximum cardinality $\mu(G)$ is a maximum matching, and a perfect matching is one saturating all vertices of $G$; a matching is almost perfect if only a single vertex is left unmatched.
elements.sent-2411-016: Given a matching $M$ in $G$, a vertex $v\in V\left( G\right) $ is $M$ -saturated if there exists an edge $e\in M$ incident with $v$.
elements.sent-2411-017: If an edge $uv$ $\in$ $E\left( G\right) $ belongs to a maximum matching, then $uv$ is matching covered.
elements.sent-2411-018: If every edge of $G$ is matching covered, then $G$ is matching covered.
elements.sent-2411-019: It is known that \begin{equation}\alpha(G)+\mu(G)\leq n\left( G\right) \leq\alpha(G)+2\mu(G)\end{equation} hold for every graph $G$ \cite{BGL2002 .}
elements.sent-2411-020: The K\"{onig deficiency of graph $G$ is $\kappa\left( G\right) =n\left( G\right) -\left( \alpha(G)+\mu(G)\right) $ \cite{BartaKres2020 .}}
elements.sent-2411-021: Thus, a graph $G$ is $k$-K\"{onig-Egerv\'{a ry if and only if $\kappa\left( G\right) =k$ \cite{LevMan2023,LevMan2024b .}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: In particular, if $k=0$, then $G$ is a K\"{onig-Egerv\'{a ry graph \cite{dem,gavril1977testing,ster .}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: K\"{o nig-Egerv\'{a ry graphs have been extensively studied \cite{Larson2007, Larson2011,LevMan2002a,LevMan2003,LevMan2006,LevMan2012a, LevMan2013b, LevMan2022,lovasz1983ear .}}}
